themes.exampleQuote: A perfect speakeasy vibe with the best cocktails I've had in Pittsburgh.
The drinks were amazing, the atmosphere was elegant, and the service was top notch.
Alicia was one of the best bartenders I've had, definitely deserves the bar manager position.
We were informed that we had to leave because there is a 90-minute time limit. This policy is only mentioned in the menu and in very small print.
I have tried to get in a few times but it is always super packed.

notable_quote: I will be making the drive from Cleveland JUST for this bar in the near future.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers consistently praise the moody botanical aesthetic, inventive cocktails, and named bartenders like Alicia and Addie; the one sharp complaint centers on a 90-minute seating limit enforced only after drinks are ordered, a policy friction that lands harder than the bar may realize.
quarter_narrative: Commerce Bar spent Spring 2026 being one of East Liberty's more talked-about rooms in person and nearly invisible everywhere else. No TikTok footprint, one Instagram post in the last 30 days, no website or phone number findable on Google: the bar that draws someone in from Cleveland on reputation alone has almost no digital surface to catch that reputation and hold it. Meanwhile, Hidden Harbor leads the Pittsburgh Bars family on social momentum, a signal Commerce Bar has essentially unplugged. The rank climbs when the Google profile closes its gaps and the Instagram account remembers it has 138 photos already worth showing the city.
tldr_read: Strong word-of-mouth and a loyal in-room following, near-zero Instagram activity and no Google website or phone, Neighborhood Staples, number two in Pittsburgh Bars.
tldr_meaning: The sentiment ceiling is real but the conversion and momentum signals are low enough to hold the rank flat until the Google profile and posting cadence catch up.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Second In Pittsburgh Bars On Reputation Alone. The Digital Profile Has Not Arrived Yet.

playbook.headline: Complete the Google Business profile now
playbook.action: Add a website URL and phone number to Google; Commerce Bar is currently missing both, which breaks the most basic conversion path for anyone who finds it in search.

playbook.headline: Revive the Instagram cadence with what already exists
playbook.action: Commerce Bar has 138 Google photos and a business Instagram account posting once in 30 days at 0.72 percent engagement; three posts a week from existing visual material would restart the momentum signal without any new production cost.

playbook.headline: Turn the cocktail names into searchable content
playbook.action: Drinks named 'absinthe makes the heart grow fonder' and 'tempting fate' are the kind of specific, quotable details that attract creator coverage; making those names findable on a menu page or pinned post gives Pittsburgh TikTok something to film without any outreach required.
